folio.label: 103r
folio.l: After my lust , and therfore wold I feyn
That thyn were , al swicú manä gouänaunce
Thow knowest eke , of olde al my plesaunce
Thougú thyn a ray be badde , and euel be seye
Do thow thyn deuer , at the leste weye
¢ Nat oonly lord , that I am glad q¶ she
To do ©oure lust , but I desire also
ñow for to serue , and pleese in my degree
Witú outen feyntyngö , and shal euäe moo
Ne neuäe for no wele , ne no woo
Ne shal the goost , witú Inne myn hert stynt
To loue ©ow best , witú al myn trewe entent
¢ And witú that word , she gan the hous to digút
And tables for to sette , and beddes make
And peyned hire , to don al that she mygút
Preyingö the chaumbreres , for goddis sake
To hasten hem , and faste swepe and shake
And she the most , seruysable of alle
Hatú euäy chaumbre arayed , and his halle
¢ A bouten the vnderne , gan this Erl a ligút
That witú hi~ brougút , theise noble children twey
For whicú the peeple , ran to se the sigút
Of here aray , so richely be sey
And tha~ne at arst , a monges hem they sey
That walter was no fool , thougú that hi~ list
To chaunge his wyf , for it was for his best
¢ For she is feyrere , as they demen alle
Than is Grisilde , and more tendre of age
And fairere fruyt , bytwen he~ shulde falle
And more plesaunt , for hire heigú lynage
Hire brother eke , so fayr was of vysage
That hem to seen , the peeple hatú caugút plesaunce
Commendyngö now , the Markys gouänaunce
¢ O stormy peeple , vnsad , and euäe vntrewe
Ay vndiscret , and chaungyngö as a fane
Delityngö euäe , in rumbul that is newe
For lyke the Mone , ay waxetú he and wane
Ay ful of clappyngö , dere I now a Iane
ñoure dome is fals , ©oure constaunce euele preuetú
A ful gret fool is he , that oon ©ow leuetú
¢ Thus seiden sadde folk , in that Citee
Whan that the peeple , gased vp and doun
For they were glad , rigút for the nouelte
To han a newe lady , of here tounö
No more of this , make I now menciouö
